public_reputation: Maintainer of quota tablets, labor rosters, and contract renewals in Unity Square.
appearance: Registrar Pol Senn carries the marks of registrar in ink, rain, and polished cloth: practical wear, maintained details, and a look that suggests they expect to be read before they are trusted.
manner: Carries the habits of a registrar whose work is visible through daily procedure, gatekeeping, and the practical pressures of Unity Square.
daily_function: Performs the ongoing duties of registrar in and around Unity Square, where the role has practical consequences even when nothing dramatic is happening.
current_agenda: Protect their footing in Unity Square while steering current strain away from a public rupture they may not survive politically or materially.
hidden_pressure: A private obligation, debt, promise, or fear tied to reputational exposure is narrowing their options faster than they admit.

adventure_hooks.summary: Registrar Pol Senn can draw the party into a practical problem centered on Unity Square.
Registrar Pol Senn becomes useful when the party needs access, explanation, or relief tied to the work of a registrar.

support_scope_notes: Created from the estate and daily-life infrastructure execution patch to make polity-native estate operations usable at table scale.
public_signal: In public, Registrar Pol Senn is readable as a registrar tied to Unity Square.

encounter_context.usual_environment: Unity Square: role-relevant halls, counters, corridors, docks, archives, shrines, workshops, gates, or administrative rooms

first_impression: Registrar Pol Senn presents as someone shaped by the duties of a registrar and the daily pressures of Unity Square.
what_they_want_from_strangers: Useful clarity, respect for the work of a registrar, and proof that strangers will not make Unity Square harder to manage.
what_they_refuse: Registrar Pol Senn refuses careless disrespect, vague promises, and demands that threaten standing or workload.
how_they_create_problems: By enforcing the expectations of a registrar too rigidly, protecting reputation, or turning a small request into a larger obligation.
how_they_can_help: Registrar Pol Senn can open local doors, explain expectations, and redirect pressure before it hardens.
escalation_if_crossed: If crossed, Registrar Pol Senn adds scrutiny, delay, and locality-specific friction before direct punishment.
rumor_about_them: People say Registrar Pol Senn always knows more about Unity Square than is immediately visible.
best_first_scene: A first encounter works best when the party needs guidance, access, or relief tied to registrar duties in Unity Square.
social_texture: Registrar Pol Senn speaks like someone who has spent too long translating private stakes into acceptable public language. Around Unity Square, people read them as socially alert, procedural, and sensitive to status.
money_pressure: Their material pressure is tied to favors and tariffs; even when wealthy, they think in terms of loss, leverage, and what one bad week would expose.
status_anxiety: What they fear most is not simple failure but reputational exposure becoming socially visible before they can contain it.
long_memory: They remember who made their work harder, who preserved dignity under strain, and who tried to force speed without bearing consequence.
obvious_tell: When pressure rises, Registrar touches clothing, tools, or insignia before answering, as if checking that their role is still intact.
best_use_case: Use Registrar Pol Senn when the party needs a socially grounded face for Unity Square: access, warning, delay, price, or reluctant help.
private_contradiction: They want to appear consistent, but their real choices are split between duty, self-protection, and preserving one relationship they cannot afford to lose.
